definition: Fiscal dynamics are a co-equal determinant of the correlation regime alongside monetary policy. US at ~6.5% deficit/GDP with ~125% debt/GDP, $2T+ annual Treasury issuance, and politically irreversible spending commitments (CHIPS/IRA, defense) define the structural difference from prior episodes.
evidence: All eight agents converge through different analytical channels — strongest cross-methodological convergence after transitional regime
Term premium at 50-80bp ACM, adjusted 80-130bp, driven by $2T+ annual issuance (rates_strategist_02)
Kalecki-Levy identity channels ~$400-600B of deficit spending into corporate profits (equity_analyst_02)
Fiscal deficits 4.5-7.0% GDP define the corridor of instability preventing regime resolution (generalist_02)
Defense spending and industrial policy are politically irreversible fiscal commitments (risk_analyst_02)
Petrodollar erosion removes price-insensitive Treasury buyers (fx_strategist_01)
Fiscal dominance makes the CB reaction function endogenous (challenger_01)
No credible fiscal consolidation mechanism exists
open_questions: Can fiscal consolidation occur without a forcing crisis?
How much of the term premium is attributable to supply vs. inflation risk vs. other factors?
Does private credit substitution ($1.5T+ AUM) materially reduce Treasury supply pressure?
What is the transmission lag from fiscal expansion to term premium?
